description:
About the job

Job Description

Are You Ready to Make It Happen at Mondelēz International?

Join our Mission to Lead the Future of Snacking. Make It Matter.

As an organization we are committed to developing the next generation of Makers and Bakers. Joining us at an early career stage in our fast paced and ever changing environment will enable you to do more, learn more and grow more. You will be encouraged to step outside your comfort zone– you may even surprise yourself! We will ensure you are given the support you need to be at your best and enable you to be yourself and bring passion and personality. Here you can lean in and speak up and bring your own flavor.

Innovation Excellence (50%):


Darás soporte al pipeline de innovación de la BU México: seguimiento de proyectos, actualización de maestros de datos y consolidación de KPIs del portafolio.
Construirás y mantendrás herramientas de análisis en Excel que apoyen la toma de decisiones del equipo de liderazgo.
Participarás en la preparación de revisiones ejecutivas (Portfolio Management Reviews) con materiales claros, estructurados y listos para audiencias VP.
Serás el/la guardián/a de la información: asegurarás que los datos del portafolio estén siempre limpios, completos y actualizados.



🎯 Culture & Capabilities (50%):


Apoyarás la ejecución de iniciativas de engagement y desarrollo de capacidades para el equipo de Marketing México.
Darás seguimiento a planes de acción, encuestas y programas de Champions internos, asegurando que nada se pierda en el camino.
Colaborarás en la logística y comunicaciones de talleres, trainings y actividades de cultura de equipo.
Serás el/la puente entre ideas y ejecución: estructurando procesos y dándoles vida.



💼 Gestión de recursos (transversal):


Gestionarás las órdenes de compra y el seguimiento presupuestal del área, trabajando con proveedores y herramientas internas de administración.
Llevarás el control del gasto vs. presupuesto, identificando desviaciones y alertando oportunamente al equipo.



Lo que buscamos en ti

No buscamos perfección; buscamos potencial. Pero si además tienes esto, definitivamente queremos conocerte:


Estudiante de Administración, Marketing, Economía, Negocios Internacionales o carrera afín (últimos semestres).
Mente analítica: te gusta trabajar con datos, encontrar patrones y convertir números en historias que importan.
Excel intermedio-avanzado: tablas dinámicas, fórmulas, consolidación de bases de datos. Si sabes algo de Power BI, es un plus que nos va a encantar.
Súper organizado/a: manejas múltiples tareas sin perder el hilo, cumples deadlines y tienes ojo para los detalles.
Comunicación clara y directa: sabes adaptar tu mensaje, ya sea en una presentación para el VP o en un Slack rápido al equipo.
Curiosidad y ganas de aprender: no temes hacer preguntas, proponer mejoras o dar una perspectiva fresca.
Inglés intermedio-avanzado (trabajamos con equipos globales).



No Relocation support available

Business Unit Summary

Mondelez México has been in the country since 1927 and currently employs 6,000 wonderful people. Our diverse portfolio includes iconic and mouth-watering global brands such as Trident, Oreo, Philadelphia, and local jewels like Clorets and Bubbaloo. We are leaders in the making of cream cheese, powdered beverages and confections—in fact, we make seven out of every 10 chewing gums consumed by Mexicans. Our growth is supported by our cutting-edge manufacturing facilities, such as our Puebla Plant and Nuevo León HUB, which are the largest gums, candies and biscuits factories in the world in terms of volume. You can buy are products in 900,000 places in Mexico. We are also home to one of the 11 technology centers Mondelez International has worldwide, a specialized gum and candy facility that places us at the forefront of innovation and development in the country and drives our purpose to lead the future of snacking. We are pioneers in the country in work-life balance practices such as extended maternity leave, open spaces, remote work and flexible working hours.

Mondelēz International is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation or preference, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Job Type

Intern (Fixed Term)

Interns

Early Careers

description:
About the job

At Roche you can show up as yourself, embraced for the unique qualities you bring. Our culture encourages personal expression, open dialogue, and genuine connections, where you are valued, accepted and respected for who you are, allowing you to thrive both personally and professionally. This is how we aim to prevent, stop and cure diseases and ensure everyone has access to healthcare today and for generations to come. Join Roche, where every voice matters.

The Position

Sobre Roche: 

Un futuro más saludable. Es lo que nos impulsa a innovar. Avanzar continuamente en la ciencia y garantizar que todos tengan acceso a la atención médica que necesitan hoy y en las generaciones futuras. Crear un mundo en el que todos tengamos más tiempo con las personas que amamos. Eso es lo que nos hace ser Roche.

Oportunidad:

Serás parte del equipo de Marketing, brindando soporte tanto estratégico como operativo en iniciativas que generen valor y vayan acorde con la estrategia de cada área terapéutica. Tendrás la oportunidad de colaborar con equipos multidisciplinarios, participar en proyectos de alto impacto y contribuir a la generación de análisis, materiales y seguimiento de iniciativas para el negocio. Será un enlace clave en la integración de diversas áreas, incluyendo equipo comercial, medical, eventos, finanzas, compliance, control interno y compras. Desarrollarás habilidades de análisis, storytelling estratégico y negociación colaborativa.

Buscamos a una persona analítica, proactiva, organizada y con interés por la industria farmacéutica, capaz de combinar pensamiento estratégico con una ejecución impecable en las actividades del día a día en entornos multifuncionales.

Responsabilidades:


Socio Estratégico del Negocio: Interactuar directamente con gerentes y coordinadores de marketing para identificar y evaluar nuevas oportunidades consistentes con los objetivos estratégicos
Gestión Operativa y Presupuesto: Coordinar actividades logísticas clave y control de procesos administrativos internos hasta su finalización. Dar seguimiento a proyectos, monitoreando cronogramas, entregables y acuerdos con las distintas áreas involucradas
Campañas de marketing: Apoyo en la creación y adaptación de materiales para comunicación interna y externa correspondientes con la estrategia del negocio y enfocado en distintas audiencias
Colaboración Multidisciplinaria: Actuar como enlace clave con el equipo de ventas, medical y diversas áreas de soporte incluyendo, eventos, finanzas, compliance, control interno y compras para consolidar información y asegurar la alineación entre stakeholders


Quién eres:


Formación: Estudiante activo(a) de los últimos semestres de Administración, Economía, Finanzas, Mercadotecnia, Negocios Internacionales, Ingeniería Industrial, Ingeniería Química, Ingeniería Biomédica u otras carreras afines relacionadas con negocios, ingeniería o ciencias
Herramientas: Manejo intermedio o avanzado de Microsoft Excel y PowerPoint. Deseable: Manejo de Google Workspace (Google Sheets, Docs, Slides, Drive y G-Site). 
Idiomas: Inglés intermedio-avanzado, con capacidad para comprender documentación técnica. Deseable: Participar en conversaciones de trabajo
Disponibilidad: Disponibilidad para laborar medio tiempo (mínimo 25 horas por semana) en horario matutino. Deseable poder asistir a la oficina tres días por semana, favoreciendo el trabajo colaborativo con el equipo (siendo indispensables los martes y miércoles para coincidir con todo el squad presencialmente en las oficinas de CDMX)


Beneficios de reubicación no están disponibles para esta posición.

Who we are

A healthier future drives us to innovate. Together, more than 100’000 employees across the globe are dedicated to advance science, ensuring everyone has access to healthcare today and for generations to come. Our efforts result in more than 26 million people treated with our medicines and over 30 billion tests conducted using our Diagnostics products. We empower each other to explore new possibilities, foster creativity, and keep our ambitions high, so we can deliver life-changing healthcare solutions that make a global impact.

Let’s build a healthier future, together.

Roche is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

The role works across Product Marketing, Digital Experience, Global Sales & Customer Success, Xcelerator Ecosystem and Marketplace, Content Strategy, Digital Engagement Center, and Data & Analytics teams to ensure digital experiences follow shared standards and support digital revenue growth.

Track funnel performance, identify drop-offs, and support optimization through testing, reporting, and continuous improvement 
Establish and follow governance standards for ecommerce execution, including product page requirements, offer structures, promotion rules, and global vs. regional execution guardrails 
Document repeatable standards, playbooks, and test-and-learn outcomes to improve predictability, speed, and quality across products and regions 
Operate with agility and a revenue-focused GTM approach, adapting quickly to business priorities while staying focused on conversion, pipeline contribution, and digital revenue impact 


Qualifications: 


Bachelor’s degree in marketing, data analytics, technology, business, CX, or related field preferred

Job Description

As part of the Innovation Team, the Project Coordinator will be responsible for coordinating and supporting strategic acquisition initiatives and operational transformation projects.

This role requires strong project management, cross-functional collaboration, and execution capabilities to ensure projects are delivered successfully, requirements are clearly defined, and implementations meet both business and operational needs.

The ideal candidate must be capable of understanding business drivers, translating them into actionable requirements, coordinating technical and operational implementations, and ensuring successful execution with minimal operational impact.

Responsibilities

Coordinate and support operational and strategic projects from planning through implementation.
Translate business needs into clear requirements and process flows.

Problem-solving and process improvement mindset.

Advanced English level.

Experience coordinating operational, digital, or technical implementations.

Familiarity with operational risk, compliance, or controls is a plus.

As part of Team Amex, you’ll experience our powerful backing with comprehensive support for your holistic well-being and many opportunities to learn new skills, develop as a leader, and grow your career. Here, your voice and ideas matter, your work makes an impact, and together, you will help us define the future of American Express.

About The Team

We back you with benefits that support your holistic well-being so you can be and deliver your best. This means caring for you and your loved ones' physical, financial, and mental health, as well as providing the flexibility you need to thrive personally and professionally:


Competitive base salaries
Bonus incentives

title: Coordinador de Influencer Marketing
skills: Identificar y seleccionar perfiles de influencers y creadores alineados con el territorio cultural de cada cuenta.
Evaluar perfiles con criterio estratégico y de datos: engagement rate, audiencia real, afinidad cultural, calidad de contenido y reputación.
Construir y mantener una base de datos de talento segmentada por industria, nicho, tamaño de audiencia y perfil cultural.
Proponer el mix de talento adecuado por campaña: macro, micro e íconos culturales según el objetivo.
Mantenerse actualizado sobre nuevos perfiles, tendencias de creadores y movimientos culturales relevantes.
Contactar, negociar y cerrar colaboraciones con influencers y creadores bajo los lineamientos de cada cuenta.
Briefear al talento con claridad: narrativa de marca, objetivos, formatos, fechas y lineamientos creativos.
Supervisar la producción de contenido del talento asegurando alineación con la narrativa y tono de la marca.
Gestionar el calendario de publicaciones, entregas y aprobaciones de cada colaboración.

Join Our Team at TalentPop App as an Influencer Marketing Specialist!

At TalentPop App, we're on a mission to transform the digital landscape through impactful influencer partnerships. We're looking for an innovative and strategic Influencer Marketing Specialist with strong marketing experience to help shape the future of e-commerce. If you're passionate about influencer marketing, skilled in executing high-impact campaigns, and eager to make a real impact, this is the perfect opportunity for you!

Why You'll Love This Role

You’ll be at the forefront of developing and executing marketing strategies that leverage influencers to build brand awareness, drive engagement, and boost conversions. As part of our team, your creativity, strategic thinking, and results-driven approach will directly contribute to our growth and success.

What You'll Do


Develop Strategies: Create influencer marketing plans that align with overall marketing objectives, brand goals, and measurable KPIs.
Source Talent: Identify and engage influencers who resonate with our brand, target audience, and campaign goals.
Build Partnerships: Cultivate and maintain strong, professional relationships with influencers, agencies, and brand partners.
Oversee Campaigns: Manage campaign timelines, content creation, and deliverables in collaboration with influencers and the marketing team.
Ensure Quality: Provide creative direction and review influencer content to ensure it aligns with brand identity and campaign messaging.
Analyze & Report: Use analytics tools to measure performance, generate reports, and optimize future marketing initiatives.
Collaborate Across Teams: Work closely with social media, content, and paid marketing teams to ensure campaigns integrate seamlessly into broader marketing efforts.


What We're Looking For


At least 1 year of proven experience in influencer marketing, with a solid background in broader marketing campaigns.
Exceptional relationship-building, negotiation, and communication skills.
In-depth understanding of social media platforms, influencer trends, digital marketing strategies, and audience engagement tactics.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret performance data and translate insights into actionable strategies.
A creative and passionate mindset for digital marketing, brand storytelling, and campaign innovation.


Tech Requirements


Personally owned PC or laptop with sufficient processing power.
Reliable internet connection with a minimum of 25 Mbps for both upload and download speed.


Perks And Benefits


Permanent remote work setup, giving you the flexibility to work from anywhere.
HMO coverage or a health stipend to support your health and well-being.
Performance bonuses to reward your hard work and success.
Generous Paid Time Off (PTO) to rest and recharge.
Opportunities for professional growth and development within a dynamic and supportive environment at TalentPop App.


Special Instruction

For us to prioritize your application for the Influencer Marketing Specialist role, please enter code INFL25 when asked.

At TalentPop App, we’re not just hiring; we’re building a team of passionate, innovative individuals who will drive the future of e-commerce. If you’re ready to make a real impact and take your marketing career to the next level, apply now!

title: Sr Associate - Consumer Digital Ecosystem
skills: Implement a comprehensive E-Commerce strategy: design, launch, and continuously optimize Lilly’s e-commerce capabilities to drive product accessibility, sell-through, and revenue growth through digital retail channels.

Launch a telehealth solution embedded in Lilly.com: lead end-to-end implementation of a telehealth capability integrated within Lilly’s digital ecosystem to extend the reach of care, facilitate patient-physician connections, and enhance the direct-to-patient value proposition.

Build and own a clear Consumer Digital Journey: map, design, and continuously improve the end-to-end digital experience for consumers, ensuring seamless interactions across all digital touchpoints from awareness to purchase and adherence.

Own the data consumption-analysis-transformation cycle: integrate and synthesize multi-sourced data (B2B, consumer, marketing, etc.) to generate actionable commercial insights, prioritize resource allocation, and inform field and consumer strategies.

Drive commercial decision-making through digital performance analytics: monitor key digital and commercial metrics (e-commerce conversion, telehealth adoption, digital journey KPIs) and adjust strategies to maximize business impact.

Lead cross-functional collaboration: partner with brand teams, commercial operations, IT, regulatory, medical affairs, and external vendors to align the digital ecosystem strategy with broader business and compliance priorities.

Manage and scale external digital partnerships: identify and cultivate relationships with technology partners, digital health start-ups, e-commerce platforms, and telehealth providers to continuously enhance Lilly’s digital consumer ecosystem.

Support brand teams in integrating digital ecosystem capabilities into their brand strategies, including identification of digital consumer segments, definition of digital-specific Critical Success Factors, and design of brand-level digital journeys.

Stay ahead of digital trends and innovation: continuously scan the competitive and technology landscape to identify emerging digital capabilities, tools, and models that can be adopted to maintain Lilly’s leadership in consumer-facing digital excellence.
At least 3 years of relevant experience in digital marketing, commercial strategy, or digital ecosystem management, with a proven track record of driving business results through digital channels.

Hands-on experience managing e-commerce platforms, telehealth solutions, or direct-to-consumer digital channels, including ownership of key digital performance metrics (conversion rates, digital funnel KPIs).

Demonstrated ability to synthesize multi-source data (B2B, consumer, commercial, marketing) into actionable insights and prioritized business decisions, with experience in data analytics tools and dashboarding.
Experience leading multi-layered projects in cross-functional and cross-national environments, including collaboration with IT, medical affairs, regulatory, and external technology vendors.
Strong commercial acumen and understanding of how digital channels (e-commerce, telehealth, direct-to-patient) translate into revenue impact and resource allocation decisions.
Strong English skills to collaborate effectively with global counterparts and multinational stakeholders. Spanish proficiency required.

Deep understanding of the digital and multi-channel marketing landscape, including SFE models, CRM tools, marketing automation platforms, and consumer journey analytics. Experience with digital media execution and paid digital strategies.
Solid project management skills with the ability to prioritize competing demands, manage vendors and external partners, and deliver results in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment.
Some travel is possible, up to 5-15%
description:
About the job

At Lilly, the work is demanding because patients are waiting. We unite caring with discovery to help make life better for people around the world, knowing that every decision, every detail, and every day matters. Headquartered in Indianapolis, Indiana, our over 50,000 employees around the globe take on complex challenges to discover and deliver life-changing medicines, strengthen how health is understood and managed, and support the communities we serve. This is hard, urgent, selfless work—but it’s work worth doing. If you’re driven by purpose and ready to bring your best to work that truly matters for patients, we invite you to join us.

Position Title: Consumer Digital Ecosystem Senior Associate

Job Level: P2 Location: Mexico

Supervisor: Joseba Barriola Potts

Consumer Digital Ecosystem is a core commercial role within the Eli Lilly Mexico Affiliate, built to translate multi-sourced data (B2B, Consumer, Marketing, and more) into prioritized commercial decisions that drive resource allocation, field execution, and consumer strategies. The position owns the full data consumption-analysis-transformation cycle and works with internal partners (brand teams, commercial teams, global teams, subject matter experts) as well as external partners (technology vendors, digital agencies, telehealth providers, e-commerce platforms) to build and scale consumer-facing digital capabilities that directly unlock new revenue channels and strengthen direct-to-patient relationships.

The Senior Associate needs deep understanding of digital ecosystems, commercial data, and consumer behavior to couple data-driven insights with business strategy and execute high-impact digital initiatives. The Senior Associate demonstrates an innate drive to innovate, a bias for action, and the ability to create seamless consumer experiences that optimize sell-through and build lasting patient relationships.

The Position

We are seeking a data-savvy, digitally-fluent, and strategically-minded individual to join our team as a Consumer Digital Ecosystem Senior Associate. In this role, you will own everything related to the digital ecosystem that directly impacts the consumer and the business — from e-commerce and telehealth to direct-to-consumer selling models and consumer digital journeys. You will be the single dedicated resource accountable for translating data into commercial decisions that optimize digital revenue channels, enhance patient relationships, and drive sell-through across an optimized digital experience.

As a Consumer Digital Ecosystem Senior Associate, your main responsibilities will be to:


Implement a comprehensive E-Commerce strategy: design, launch, and continuously optimize Lilly’s e-commerce capabilities to drive product accessibility, sell-through, and revenue growth through digital retail channels.
Launch a telehealth solution embedded in Lilly.com: lead end-to-end implementation of a telehealth capability integrated within Lilly’s digital ecosystem to extend the reach of care, facilitate patient-physician connections, and enhance the direct-to-patient value proposition.
Build and own a clear Consumer Digital Journey: map, design, and continuously improve the end-to-end digital experience for consumers, ensuring seamless interactions across all digital touchpoints from awareness to purchase and adherence.
Own the data consumption-analysis-transformation cycle: integrate and synthesize multi-sourced data (B2B, consumer, marketing, etc.) to generate actionable commercial insights, prioritize resource allocation, and inform field and consumer strategies.
Drive commercial decision-making through digital performance analytics: monitor key digital and commercial metrics (e-commerce conversion, telehealth adoption, digital journey KPIs) and adjust strategies to maximize business impact.
Lead cross-functional collaboration: partner with brand teams, commercial operations, IT, regulatory, medical affairs, and external vendors to align the digital ecosystem strategy with broader business and compliance priorities.
Manage and scale external digital partnerships: identify and cultivate relationships with technology partners, digital health start-ups, e-commerce platforms, and telehealth providers to continuously enhance Lilly’s digital consumer ecosystem.
Support brand teams in integrating digital ecosystem capabilities into their brand strategies, including identification of digital consumer segments, definition of digital-specific Critical Success Factors, and design of brand-level digital journeys.
Stay ahead of digital trends and innovation: continuously scan the competitive and technology landscape to identify emerging digital capabilities, tools, and models that can be adopted to maintain Lilly’s leadership in consumer-facing digital excellence.



Qualifications

About You


At least 3 years of relevant experience in digital marketing, commercial strategy, or digital ecosystem management, with a proven track record of driving business results through digital channels.
Hands-on experience managing e-commerce platforms, telehealth solutions, or direct-to-consumer digital channels, including ownership of key digital performance metrics (conversion rates, digital funnel KPIs).
Demonstrated ability to synthesize multi-source data (B2B, consumer, commercial, marketing) into actionable insights and prioritized business decisions, with experience in data analytics tools and dashboarding.
Experience leading multi-layered projects in cross-functional and cross-national environments, including collaboration with IT, medical affairs, regulatory, and external technology vendors.
Strong commercial acumen and understanding of how digital channels (e-commerce, telehealth, direct-to-patient) translate into revenue impact and resource allocation decisions.
Strong English skills to collaborate effectively with global counterparts and multinational stakeholders. Spanish proficiency required.
Deep understanding of the digital and multi-channel marketing landscape, including SFE models, CRM tools, marketing automation platforms, and consumer journey analytics. Experience with digital media execution and paid digital strategies.
Solid project management skills with the ability to prioritize competing demands, manage vendors and external partners, and deliver results in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment.



Location: Mexico

Additional Information


Some travel is possible, up to 5-15%

Garantizar altos niveles de satisfacción y experiencia del cliente, dando seguimiento a la calidad del servicio, atendiendo requerimientos y resolviendo incidencias oportunamente, para fomentar la retención de clientes, la reputación del negocio y la recurrencia de compra.

Monitorear tendencias del mercado automotriz de seminuevos y el comportamiento de la competencia, a través de análisis de información de mercado, benchmarking y retroalimentación de clientes, para anticiparse a cambios del entorno, ajustar estrategias comerciales y mantener la competitividad del negocio.

description:
About the job

¡En Localiza&Co estamos en busca de personas entusiastas para unirse a nuestro equipo como Key Account Seminuevos en México! Nuestro objetivo es formar un equipo de talento apasionado por las ventas y relaciones comerciales a través de altos estándares de negociación para líneas de negocio corporativas.




Objetivo de la posición:

Planificar, dirigir y supervisar las estrategias comerciales de venta de vehículos en una región determinada, asegurando el cumplimiento de metas de ventas, rentabilidad, participación de mercado y satisfacción del cliente.

 

 Principales actividades:

Definir, diseñar e implementar estrategias comerciales para el segmento de cuentas clave en seminuevos, a través del análisis de objetivos corporativos, desempeño histórico de ventas, comportamiento del mercado y colaboración con áreas internas, para asegurar el crecimiento sostenido de ventas, la participación de mercado y la alineación con los objetivos estratégicos del negocio.
Garantizar altos niveles de satisfacción y experiencia del cliente, dando seguimiento a la calidad del servicio, atendiendo requerimientos y resolviendo incidencias oportunamente, para fomentar la retención de clientes, la reputación del negocio y la recurrencia de compra.
Monitorear tendencias del mercado automotriz de seminuevos y el comportamiento de la competencia, a través de análisis de información de mercado, benchmarking y retroalimentación de clientes, para anticiparse a cambios del entorno, ajustar estrategias comerciales y mantener la competitividad del negocio.
Establecer metas de venta para las cuentas asignadas y monitorea su cumplimiento, mediante la definición de KPIs, elaboración de planes comerciales y seguimiento periódico del desempeño de cada cuenta, para garantizar el logro de objetivos de volumen, ingresos y rentabilidad del canal.
Analizar indicadores clave del negocio como ventas, margen, inventario y rotación, utilizando herramientas analíticas, reportes comerciales y dashboards de desempeño, para identificar oportunidades, riesgos y áreas de mejora que permitan optimizar los resultados comerciales.
Diseñar e implementar planes de acción orientados a incrementar ventas y mejorar el desempeño de cuentas clave, a partir de diagnósticos comerciales, detección de brechas de desempeño y coordinación con equipos internos, para maximizar la productividad de las cuentas y asegurar el cumplimiento de los objetivos establecidos.
 

 




 Requisitos mínimos:

 • Licenciatura en Administración, Mercadotecnia, Negocios o afín. Concluida (con título/cédula o carta pasante) 

 • Experiencia mínima de 3 a 5 años en ventas (preferentemente en sector automotriz)

• Experiencia en estrategias comerciales

• Manejo de indicadores clave del negocio como ventas, margen, inventario y rotación

 • Excel avanzado y paquetería Office, o uso de CRM.

 • Excelentes habilidades de comunicación, negociación y persuasión. Manejo de socios comerciales

• Disponibilidad de horario

• Disponibilidad para viajar dentro de la región

• Licencia vigente, manejo de auto estándar y automático

 

Esquema de trabajo: 6 Dias de trabajo (lunes a sábado). Horario de 9:00 hrs a 18:00 hrs ó 10:00 hrs a 19:00 hrs




Ubicación: México, Monterrey y Cancún 




Ofrecemos:

 • Sueldo competitivo

• Prestaciones superiores a las de la Ley




En Localiza serás protagonista de tu desarrollo en un ambiente de oportunidades, aprendizaje y crecimiento constante.

About the job

About WPP Media

Be Extraordinary by Leading Collectively to Inspire transformational Creativity.
Create an Open environment by Balancing People and Client Experiences by Cultivating Trust.
Lead Optimistically by Championing Growth and Development to Mobilize the Enterprise.


Role Summary And Impact

As part of the Global Operations Centre (GOC), you will work closely with teams across the United States, Europe, and Asia to deliver high-impact solutions in a collaborative, multicultural environment. We are looking for a Senior Executive Social who will:


Develop strong relationships with counterparts in WPP Media’s regional teams (Europe, United States and Asia). 
Support campaigns on Facebook Ads, Twitter Ads, LinkedIn Ads, Instagram Ads, etc., for clients, according to WPP Media guidelines. 
Ensure campaigns comply with WPP Media’s global policies and guidelines. 
Monitor campaigns daily on each of the platforms and ensure accuracy of data across platforms for campaign reporting. 
Generate daily campaign reports for reporting to regional teams. 


Skills And Experience

If this opportunity looks right for you, we are looking for candidates who have:


Bachelor’s degree.
Advanced English level (B2–C2).
Have 1.5-2 years of implementing and optimizing paid social campaigns in platforms such as: Facebook Ads, LinkedIn Ads, Instagram Ads, Twitter Ads, etc.
Advanced Excel skills. 


Life At WPP Media & Benefits

Our passion for shaping the next era of media includes investing in our employees to help them do their best work, and we’re just as committed to employee growth as we are to responsible media investment. WPP Media employees can tap into the global WPP Media & WPP networks to pursue their passions, grow their networks, and learn at the cutting edge of marketing and advertising. We have a variety of employee resource groups and host frequent in-office events showcasing team wins, sharing thought leadership, and celebrating holidays and milestone events. Our benefits include access to internal learning platforms, certifications, and training sessions, life insurance, wellness programs, significant paid time off, preferential partner discounts, employee mental health awareness day, and more.

WPP Media is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

description:
About the job

Paid Media Buyer — Multi-Platform | Brewery & eCommerce Brands

Talent Global LLC — Remote

Full-Time, 40 hrs/week | EST | Long-Term Contract




About the Role

Our client is a US-based boutique digital marketing agency that manages paid media campaigns for brewery, beverage, and eCommerce brands across the United States. The team is scaling fast and needs a hands-on Media Buyer to take ownership of campaign execution across Meta, Google, TikTok, and Pinterest for an active portfolio of client accounts. This is the right role for a mid-level buyer who is data-driven, works independently, and is ready to grow in a fast-paced agency environment.




Key Responsibilities

Plan, launch, and optimize paid media campaigns across Meta (primary), Google, TikTok, and Pinterest
Manage brewery and eCommerce client accounts with full day-to-day campaign ownership
Install, configure, and troubleshoot Pixel events and conversion tracking across client websites
Build and test audience segments: interest-based, lookalike, retargeting, and custom audiences
Analyze performance data (ROAS, CPA, CPM, CTR) and make optimization decisions without heavy oversight
Report on campaign results with clear insights and actionable next steps
Stay current on platform updates, algorithm changes, and paid media best practices
Communicate proactively with internal team on account status, priorities, and strategy




Requirements

Minimum 3 years of hands-on paid media experience
Meta Ads as primary platform — mandatory
Working knowledge of at least one additional platform: Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or Pinterest Ads
Ability to independently install and test Pixel events and conversion tracking
Experience managing multiple client accounts simultaneously
Self-starter — able to operate without constant direction
C1+ English, written and verbal
Available full-time Monday–Friday aligned with US Eastern Time (EST)




Nice to Have

Experience with brewery, beverage, hospitality, or local business marketing
eCommerce or DTC campaign background
Familiarity with attribution tools (Triple Whale, Northbeam, or similar)
Experience with local and radius-based targeting




Ideal Candidate

This role is built for someone who has built a real foundation in Meta Ads and is expanding into multi-platform buying. They don't need to be told what to test or optimize — they read the data and move. The ideal candidate is comfortable owning accounts in an agency setting where multiple clients and verticals are running at the same time. Experience with brewery or local business marketing is a plus, but a solid eCommerce background transfers directly. If you're mid-level and want to grow in a results-driven, entrepreneurial agency, this is your environment.

About The Role

This team collaborates with a diverse network of content creators, media partners, and comparison platforms to deliver high-quality, compliant content that educates consumers while generating measurable business results.

As the Affiliate Marketing Program continues to scale, this role will play a key part in enabling data-driven decision-making, improving partner performance, and ensuring operational excellence. You will support performance analysis, campaign optimization, and partner engagement while contributing to the growth and effectiveness of a performance-based marketing model.

This is an exciting opportunity for a data-oriented marketing professional who enjoys working cross-functionally, optimizing campaigns, and contributing to a growing, results-driven program.

What You'll Do


Analyze affiliate partner performance to identify opportunities to improve efficiency across traffic sources and lines of business (LOB)
Validate performance results, monitor for anomalies or potential fraud, and ensure compliance with program standards
Support the optimization of marketing campaigns through data analysis and testing (including A/B testing initiatives)
Assist in managing relationships with affiliate partners, providing day-to-day support and ensuring alignment with program goals
Contribute to campaign planning and execution, including content initiatives such as newsletters and partner communications
Create and analyze business-to-consumer marketing content (email, direct mail, digital assets) to improve engagement and conversion rates
Support business-to-business marketing initiatives by coordinating with stakeholders across sales, legal, product, and external partners
Manage timelines and deliverables for marketing initiatives, ensuring on-time and high-quality execution
Leverage data, analytics, and AI-driven insights to refine strategies and enhance marketing performance
Partner with cross-functional teams (marketing, analytics, technology) to improve tracking, reporting, and campaign effectiveness
Develop and execute test-and-learn strategies to continuously improve performance and customer experience
Stay up to date with industry trends and best practices in affiliate and digital marketing


Requirements


3-5 years of experience in Digital Marketing, Affiliate Marketing, or Performance Marketing
Bachelor's degree
Experience working with agency partners, publishers, or third-party marketing platforms is a plus
Microsoft Excel (Intermediate) 
Power BI (Entry Level)
Experience leveraging data and analytics to drive marketing decisions
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and translate insights into actionable recommendations
Detail-oriented, with a focus on accuracy, compliance, and performance validation
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills

Exposure to campaign testing (A/B testing) and optimization strategies

Comprehensive benefits package
Career development and training opportunities

description:
About the job

Resumen del rol

Buscamos a la persona que va a construir la función de producto de PoderJudicialVirtual y llevarla rumbo a nuestro plan 2030: pasar de 2 productos en producción a 11, y expandirnos a Sudamérica.




Hoy el único Product Manager es el CEO, y ese es el cuello de botella. Tú lo resuelves: escribes specs, decides microcopy, priorizas backlog, coordinas releases con el equipo en India y en México, y poco a poco tomas ownership real de los productos en producción. Es un rol hands-on, de construir, no de solo dirigir, y se baja al nivel del botón. Aquí escribir el microcopy y dejar la documentación impecable es parte importante del trabajo.




Es, ante todo, un rol de producto: qué construir y por qué, discovery, specs, métricas de producto. La coordinación operativa con devs, QA y vendors es parte del trabajo, pero el producto es el centro. Esto es product management, no project management, ni PMO, ni delivery.




Eres la primera persona de producto del equipo. Si construyes bien, conforme PJV crezca hacia 2030 serás la persona natural para liderar la función de producto que tú ayudaste a montar. Aquí el crecimiento se gana construyendo, no por antigüedad ni por un título prometido de antemano.




Buscamos ambición de montar desde cero, más que de gestionar. Si tu mejor versión es dueña de un producto y no de un organigrama, este lugar es para ti.




Trabajarás de la mano de un líder técnico fuerte, dueño de la arquitectura y la infraestructura. Tú eres dueño del producto, el proceso y la prioridad.




Vas a aprender un dominio complejo (infraestructura judicial mexicana, scraping de portales legales, OCR, notificaciones legales) que tarda meses en dominarse. No esperamos que llegues sabiéndolo. Sí esperamos que en 12 meses tomes decisiones independientes en áreas conocidas y aportes ideas que mejoren las del fundador.




Sobre nosotros

PoderJudicialVirtual es una empresa legaltech mexicana con más de 10 años en el mercado. Operamos dos productos en producción (notificaciones judiciales y búsquedas de antecedentes legales) y tenemos varios en desarrollo, con la meta de llegar a 11 productos en producción para 2030 y expandirnos a Sudamérica.




Somos una empresa pequeña, rentable, autofinanciada y remote-first. Vendemos por producto, de forma self-service e inbound desde el sitio; el CEO cierra directo las cuentas enterprise. La operación es ligera, las decisiones son rápidas y trabajamos con IA en todo lo que hacemos. Acabamos de lanzar el rediseño completo de la plataforma y estamos en el momento de ponerle gasolina al crecimiento.




Lo que vas a hacer




Producto (70% del tiempo)

Escribir specs claros para los productos en producción y los nuevos lanzamientos. Empiezas en coautoría con el CEO y poco a poco tomas ownership.
Tomar decisiones rápidas de UX, microcopy y comportamiento.
Priorizar backlog por producto, con base en datos de uso, conversión, ingresos y feedback de clientes.
Coordinar releases: validar antes de producción, manejar el rollout, comunicar a soporte y a clientes cuando aplique.
Documentar decisiones y mantener el estado del mundo actualizado: qué está en desarrollo, qué se aprobó, qué se descartó y por qué.
Analizar métricas con GA4, dashboards internos y datos de Stripe, y proponer mejoras a partir de lo que ves.




Operaciones (20% del tiempo)

Coordinar al equipo de India: traducir specs, dar seguimiento al avance, resolver bloqueos. La comunicación es 80% asíncrona y por escrito.
Acompañar el QA actual y, eventualmente, ayudar a onboardear al QA SDET cuando se contrate.
Mantener la documentación operativa al día (procesos, runbooks, guías internas).




Coordinación con devs locales (10% del tiempo)

Reuniones de avance con el equipo en México (líder técnico y developers): llevar agenda, dar seguimiento, identificar bloqueos.
Servir de puente para resolver decisiones menores.




Perfil que buscamos




Mínimos no negociables

5+ años como Product Manager de producto digital (idealmente B2B SaaS, no consumer).
Experiencia construyendo producto o procesos desde cero (0→1), en entorno lean y con ambigüedad. No solo operar dentro de un sistema ya montado, sino montarlo: de la idea a la spec, al Definition of Ready, a Jira, al Definition of Done con QA. Es el corazón de este rol rumbo a 2030.
Has escrito specs que un dev pudo ejecutar sin volver a preguntarte cinco veces lo mismo. Tienes portfolio o ejemplos para mostrar.
Sabes leer datos cuantitativos (analytics, embudo de conversión, retención) y construir hipótesis a partir de ellos.
Eres autónoma/o en remoto. No necesitas que alguien te marque las tareas del día.
Usas IA todo el día (escribir, investigar, analizar, prototipar) para amplificar tu pensamiento, no para sustituirlo.
Capacidad de bajar al nivel del botón. No es un rol solo estratégico: hay que escribir microcopy y decidir si el botón va a la izquierda o a la derecha.




Lo que nos va a enamorar

Tienes opinión propia y la defiendes con datos o argumentos. Cuando se te pide algo que crees que está mal, lo dices.
Conoces el dominio legal o judicial mexicano (no es requisito, pero suma mucho).
Has trabajado con equipos distribuidos, especialmente con India o husos horarios complicados.
Tienes algún proyecto donde construiste algo de cero (no necesariamente código): un blog, un newsletter, una comunidad, un side-project que vendiste, lo que sea.




Idiomas

Español nativo. Inglés escrito B2 o superior, filtro duro que se evalúa en el proceso: tienes que poder leer specs en inglés, escribir tickets en Trello y Jira en inglés, y sostener comunicación asíncrona en inglés con el equipo de developers. No necesitamos fluidez conversacional; la mayoría de la comunicación con India es por escrito.




Lo que ofrecemos

Sueldo base entre $65,000 y $80,000 MXN mensuales brutos, según experiencia y nivel.
Esquema de contratación según tu ubicación: relación laboral formal en México (prestaciones de ley y reparto de utilidades) o esquema de honorarios como contractor para candidatos en LATAM fuera de México.
Bono anual aproximado de 2 a 3 meses, según resultados de la empresa y desempeño individual.
100% remoto.
Crecimiento real, ganado construyendo: eres la persona que va a montar la función de producto, y conforme PJV escale hacia 2030 puedes crecer a liderar el equipo de producto que ayudaste a crear.
Trabajamos con estructura, no con micromanagement: medimos resultados.




Proceso de selección

Diseñado para identificar pensamiento de producto real, no memoria de frameworks. Cinco etapas:




Etapa 1: Aplicación inicial

Junto con tu CV, mándanos dos respuestas cortas:

(Español, máximo una cuartilla) Revisa nuestro producto (poderjudicialvirtual.com) por al menos 20 minutos. ¿Qué tres cosas cambiarías y por qué? No buscamos la respuesta correcta, buscamos cómo piensas.
(En inglés, un par de párrafos) Cuéntanos de una vez que construiste un producto o un proceso desde cero: qué no existía, qué montaste y cómo se veía seis meses después.




Etapa 2: Caso operativo

Si tu aplicación pasa el filtro, te mandamos un caso corto por escrito y tienes 48 horas para responder. Evaluamos: ownership, criterio de urgencia, comunicación con cliente, escalamiento al equipo correcto y documentación post-incidente.




Etapa 3: Entrevista

Una conversación de 60 a 90 minutos. Profundizamos en tus respuestas de las etapas anteriores y en tu trayectoria.




Etapa 4: Mini-test de inglés escrito

Te mandamos un spec corto en español y te pedimos reescribirlo en inglés como si fuera un ticket de Jira para developers no hispanohablantes. 30 minutos máximo. Validamos que el nivel B2 escrito está cubierto.




Etapa 5: Oferta y referencias

Si todo va bien, hacemos una oferta y solicitamos 2 a 3 referencias profesionales.




Cómo aplicar

Mándanos a talento@poderjudicialvirtual.com:

Tu CV.
La respuesta en español (máximo una cuartilla) a las tres cosas que cambiarías de poderjudicialvirtual.com y por qué.
La respuesta en inglés (un par de párrafos) sobre algo que construiste desde cero.

description:
About the job

About Remote

Remote is solving modern organizations’ biggest challenge – navigating global employment compliantly with ease. We make it possible for businesses of all sizes to recruit, pay, and manage international teams. With our core values at heart and future focused work culture, our team works tirelessly on ambitious problems, asynchronously, around the world. You can find Remoters working from 6 different continents (Antarctica left to go!) and all of our positions are fully remote.

With Innovation as one of the core values, we have built Automation and AI capabilities into the requirements for every role.

We encourage every member of the Remote team to bring their talents, experiences and culture to the table to help us build the best-in-class HR platform.

If you are energetic, curious, motivated and ambitious, be part of our world. Apply now and define the future of work!

About Remote

Remote is solving the biggest challenge in modern business: building and scaling global teams. We enable companies to employ anyone, anywhere compliantly, while simplifying payroll, benefits, taxes, and HR. Our mission is to create opportunity everywhere — and storytelling plays a critical role in making that real.

What You’ll Do

Define and evolve Remote’s brand voice

Shape a distinctive, consistent voice that reflects Remote’s clarity, confidence, and global perspective. Establish tone and editorial standards that scale across campaigns and our website.

Own creative campaign storytelling end-to-end

Lead messaging and writing for creative campaigns — from big ideas and narratives to execution across channels. Develop work that is memorable, differentiated, and deeply aligned with our audience.

Elevate the Remote website

Write and optimize high-impact website content that clearly communicates our value, simplifies complex topics, and drives conversion. Continuously refine messaging based on performance and audience insight.

Create scalable content systems

Develop repeatable formats, messaging frameworks, and modular content that can be reused and adapted across campaigns and web experiences.

Turn attention into action

Craft content that doesn’t just inform — it moves people. Help transform visitors into customers, and customers into advocates through compelling, conversion-oriented storytelling.

Experiment and push the brand forward

Explore new formats, channels, and creative approaches. Bring fresh, modern storytelling techniques into Remote’s brand ecosystem.

Scale with AI (thoughtfully)

Leverage AI to increase speed, consistency, and creative ambition — while maintaining a strong editorial point of view and human quality bar.

Collaborate across Remote

Partner with marketing, product, design, and leadership to bring sharp, differentiated perspectives to life. Translate complex global employment topics into clear, engaging narratives.

Raise the bar for brand writing

Set a high standard for craft, clarity, and creativity. Continuously improve how we tell Remote’s story across every touchpoint.

What You Bring


Proven experience writing high-quality brand and marketing content across formats — especially creative campaigns and web
Exceptional writing and editing skills, with a strong sense of voice, tone, and narrative structure
Strong editorial judgment, including knowing what not to create
Experience translating complex topics into clear, engaging, and accessible content
Deep understanding of modern content patterns (clarity, structure, flow, and conversion principles)
A clear perspective on how AI can enhance content creation
Track record of driving measurable impact through content (brand, engagement, and conversion)
Ability to collaborate cross-functionally and influence stakeholders
Experience in HR tech, SaaS, or fintech — or strong curiosity about the future of work and global employment



Why This Role Matters

This role sits at the heart of how Remote shows up in the world. You’ll help define how we tell our story — making complex global employment feel simple, human, and compelling. Your work will shape first impressions, drive growth, and build a brand that companies trust as they expand globally.

Application process


Interview with recruiter
Interview with future manager
Interview with team members 
Bar Raiser Interview
Prior employment verification check 



Remote's Total Rewards philosophy is to ensure fair, unbiased compensation and fair equity pay along with competitive benefits in all locations in which we operate. We do not agree to or encourage cheap-labor practices and therefore we ensure to pay above in-location rates. We hope to inspire other companies to support global talent-hiring and bring local wealth to developing countries.

At first glance our salary bands seem quite wide - here is some context. At Remote we have international operations and a globally distributed workforce. We use geo ranges to consider geographic pay differentials as part of our global compensation strategy to remain competitive in various markets while we hiring globally.

Our salary ranges are determined by role, level and location, and our job titles may span more than one career level. The actual base pay for the successful candidate in this role is dependent upon many factors such as location, transferable or job-related skills, work experience, relevant training, business needs, and market demands. The base salary range may be subject to change.

At Remote, we foster internal mobility as a key element of our culture of employee growth and development, supported by a compensation philosophy that guarantees pay equity and fairness. Therefore, all compensation changes associated with an internal move will be reviewed by the Total Rewards & People Enablement team on a case by case basis.

The annual salary range for this full-time position is

$90,000—$120,000 USD

Benefits

Our full benefits & perks are explained in our handbook at remote.com/r/benefits. As a global company, each country works differently, but some benefits/perks are for all Remoters:


work from anywhere
flexible paid time off
flexible working hours (we are async)
16 weeks paid parental leave
mental health support services
stock options
learning budget
home office budget & IT equipment
budget for local in-person social events or co-working spaces



How You’ll Plan Your Day (and Life)

We work async at Remote which means you can plan your schedule around your life (and not around meetings). Read more at remote.com/async.

You will be empowered to take ownership and be proactive. When in doubt you will default to action instead of waiting. Your life-work balance is important and you will be encouraged to put yourself and your family first, and fit work around your needs.

If that sounds like something you want, apply now!

How To Apply


Please fill out the form below and upload your CV with a PDF format.
We kindly ask you to submit your application and CV in English, as this is the standardised language we use here at Remote.
If you don’t have an up to date CV but you are still interested in talking to us, please feel free to add a copy of your LinkedIn profile instead.



Not only do we encourage folks from all ethnic groups, genders, sexuality, age, abilities, disability status and any other under-represented group to apply, but we prioritize a sense of belonging. We have 4 ERGs (Women, Disability, Queer, Minorities in Tech) who meet regularly with the People team. During your interviews and beyond, we ask & encourage anybody who needs an accommodation to request one from their recruiter.

We will ask you to voluntarily tell us your pronouns at interview stage, and you will have the option to answer our anonymous demographic questionnaire when you apply below. As an equal employment opportunity employer it’s important to us that our workforce reflects people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences and this data will help us to stay accountable. We thank you for providing this data, if you chose to.

At Remote, we embrace AI as a valuable tool while prioritizing human creativity and authenticity. We look forward to meeting candidates who balance innovation with genuine expertise and experience. To learn more about Remote's AI guidelines check see here.

Please note we accept applications on an ongoing basis.

Collaborate closely with product managers, designers, and fellow engineers to deliver high-impact features that meet the needs of customers end to end.
Design, implement, and maintain scalable and reliable backend microservices for EarnIn’s payroll products.
Investigate and resolve production issues across the stack with a focus on root cause analysis, long-term stability, and continuous improvement.

description:
About the job

About EarnIn

As one of the first pioneers of earned wage access, our passion at EarnIn is building products that deliver real-time financial flexibility for those with the unique needs of living paycheck to paycheck. Our community members access their earnings as they earn them, with options to spend, save, and grow their money without mandatory fees, interest rates, or credit checks.

We’re fortunate to have an incredibly experienced leadership team, combined with world-class funding partners like A16Z, Matrix Partners, DST, Ribbit Capital, and a very healthy core business with a tremendous runway. We’re growing fast and are excited to continue bringing world-class talent onboard to help shape the next chapter of our growth journey.

 

POSITION SUMMARY 

We are looking for an experienced, passionate, and resourceful senior-level engineer with a proven track record to join the EarnIn payroll services team. In this role, you will participate in the design, development, and implementation of both backend services and the frontend experiences that power EarnIn’s new time & attendance product. You’ll work across the stack to ensure backend systems and user-facing applications are well-aligned, intuitive, and reliable.

Come make a difference with us and have fun doing it! This position is ideally hybrid from our Mexico City office as part of our expanding site, though remote can be considered. EarnIn offers excellent benefits, including healthcare, internet and cell phone reimbursement, a learning and development stipend, and potential opportunities to travel to our Mountain View, CA headquarters. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Design & build key components for EarnIn’s time-and-attendance system.
Develop robust, secure, and scalable backend services and APIs (Golang) while contributing to intuitive, accessible user-facing frontend (ReactJS) features for our new time & attendance product.
Collaborate closely with product managers, designers, and fellow engineers to deliver high-impact features that meet the needs of customers end to end.
Design, implement, and maintain scalable and reliable backend microservices for EarnIn’s payroll products.
Investigate and resolve production issues across the stack with a focus on root cause analysis, long-term stability, and continuous improvement.
Contribute to building a modern, best-in-class platform that handles time-critical workflows with precision and reliability.
Conduct code reviews to ensure code quality, adherence to best practices, maintainability, and alignment with project requirements, while providing constructive feedback to team members across backend and frontend codebases.
Ensure the delivery of high-quality software features within established timelines.

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR 

4+ years of development experience in a fast-paced environment.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, computer engineering, or a related technical discipline, or equivalent industry experience.
Experienced in building and maintaining end-to-end web applications using modern backend languages (e.g., Go, C#, Python) and frontend frameworks (e.g., React, TypeScript), with a strong focus on scalable architecture and user-centric design. 
Hands-on experience working with relational databases.
Cloud experience, AWS preferred.
Experience managing infrastructure and service configurations using Configuration as Code (e.g., Helm, Kustomize, Terraform).
Deploy and operate backend services in a Kubernetes-based environment, optimizing for resiliency, scalability, and performance.
Experience using AI-assisted development tools (e.g., GitHub Copilot, Cursor, ChatGPT, or similar tools) as part of your software development workflow
Comfort collaborating across the stack, including working closely with design and product to translate requirements into user-facing functionality.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment with a strong bias toward action and results.
 

At EarnIn, we believe that the best way to build a financial system that works for everyday people is by hiring a team that represents our diverse community. Our team is diverse not only in background and experience but also in perspective. We celebrate our diversity and strive to create a culture of belonging. EarnIn does not unlawfully discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), gender identity, gender expression, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, age, physical or mental disability, legally protected medical condition, family care status, military or veteran status, marital status, registered domestic partner status, sexual orientation, genetic information, or any other basis protected by local, state, or federal laws. EarnIn is an E-Verify participant. 

EarnIn does not accept unsolicited resumes from individual recruiters or third-party recruiting agencies in response to job postings. No fee will be paid to third parties who submit unsolicited candidates directly to our hiring managers or HR team.

Who We Are

Quiénes Somos

Burson forma parte de WPP (LSE/NYSE: WPP), un referente mundial en transformación creativa. Para más información, visite bursonglobal.com y síganos en LinkedIn e Instagram.

Tu Lugar Está En Burson

Nuestra visión es que Burson sea la principal ‘academy company’ para profesionales creativos de la comunicación y líderes emergentes. Ofrecemos un entorno dinámico donde los colaboradores vienen a potenciar sus habilidades, ampliar sus redes y, en última instancia, crear las mejores soluciones para las marcas líderes de hoy. Nuestro compromiso con su crecimiento se refleja en nuestros sólidos programas de beneficios y programas para el talento, que incluyen oportunidades de desarrollo profesional, programas de mentoría con líderes de la industria, una cultura apasionante e inclusiva que fomenta la colaboración y la innovación, así como programas para recompensar y retener a los mejores talentos. Por eso es fundamental para nosotros contratar a personas extraordinariamente talentosas, con diversos contextos culturales e identidades. Sin ello, no podríamos realizar el trabajo esencial de inspirar las ideas más impactantes, fomentar diálogos constructivos, cultivar experiencias significativas e impulsar la innovación y la creatividad para nuestros socios. description:
About the job

About Page Vault

At Page Vault, we're on a mission to transform how web content is used in the legal industry. Our technology captures online evidence effortlessly - and critically, makes it admissible in court. We serve law firms, litigation support teams, and in-house legal departments who rely on us when it matters most. We're a fully remote team spread across the U.S., driven by a passion for solving hard problems for clients who can't afford to get it wrong.

The Role

We're looking for a Customer Support Specialist who takes real pride in the client experience - someone who doesn't consider a ticket closed until the client actually understands what happened and feels taken care of.

This isn't a role where you paste canned responses and move on. Our clients are legal professionals dealing with case-critical work. They need a support contact who listens carefully, communicates clearly, and follows through. You'll be the person they trust when something's urgent or unclear.

What You'll Do

Be the first line of support clients count on


Respond to client inquiries via email, chat, and phone with clarity and care
Troubleshoot and resolve issues, guiding clients through solutions step by step
Investigate and reproduce reported issues to understand root cause before escalating
Gather the right context and escalate technical or product issues with clean documentation — so engineering and product aren't starting from scratch


Own your queue like it matters


Manage and prioritize incoming requests to meet response and resolution expectations
Handle escalated situations with professionalism and a steady hand
Cover the client emergency line


Make things better over time


Spot patterns in recurring issues and surface insights to help improve the product and the client experience
Lead client training sessions as needed
Collaborate with product, engineering, and operations to close the loop on complex issues


This Role Might Be For You If…


You'd rather over-communicate than leave a client wondering what's happening
You find yourself genuinely curious about why something broke, not just how to close the ticket
You can translate a confusing technical situation into a clear, calm explanation
You take follow-through seriously: when you say you'll get back to someone, you do
You're comfortable managing a busy queue without letting anything slip through the cracks
You're energized by variety - every request is a little different, and you like it that way


Requirements


1+ years of experience in customer support, client services, or technical support
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Strong troubleshooting instincts; you dig into problems rather than deflecting them
Ability to quickly learn new software tools and workflows
Comfortable managing multiple open requests and reprioritizing on the fly
Available to work 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST, Monday–Friday. Note: the first two months require availability from ~9:00 AM – 6:00 PM ET for training; exact timing is confirmed during the interview process.


Nice to Have


Experience in the legal or e-discovery industry
Familiarity with HubSpot or similar CRM/support tools
Experience in a high-touch B2B SaaS environment


Benefits

Benefits include but are not limited to:


Fully remote work, with 2-3 in-person team meetings per year
Flexible PTO, unlimited sick days, and paid parental leave for both parents
Medical, dental, and vision insurance
401(k) with employer contribution
Base salary range: $45,000-$55,000, depending on experience. Compensation is base-weighted with a modest performance-based variable component.

About the job

CUSTOMER SUPPORT SPECIALIST (B2B)

Full-time (40hrs per week) / Remote

Check out The Global Talent Co.’s talent network: https://www.globaltalent.co/network

The role:

You will work with a fast-growing company that delivers ready-to-heat meal solutions to organizations across corporate offices, healthcare facilities, schools, and residential communities. Unlike traditional customer support, a single issue can impact hundreds of meals, multiple stakeholders, and critical service commitments, making accuracy, responsiveness, and operational excellence essential to success.

As a Client Support Specialist, you will serve as the frontline of the Customer Experience team, managing client inquiries, processing orders, maintaining customer accounts, and resolving operational issues while delivering an exceptional customer experience.

Position: CUSTOMER SUPPORT SPECIALIST B2B (Full-Time)

Seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented Client Support Specialist to support business clients through order management, customer communication, and operational issue resolution. The ideal candidate has experience working in high-volume customer support environments, thrives in fast-paced operations, and takes full ownership of customer outcomes.

Responsibilities:

Order Support & Processing


Perform order entry, modifications, and account updates using Shopify and internal systems. 
Process remakes, replacements, and credit requests accurately while meeting established SLAs. 
Investigate missing, damaged, incorrect, or delayed deliveries and coordinate appropriate resolutions. 
Monitor active orders and proactively communicate updates to customers. 
Partner with Logistics, Fulfillment, and Operations teams to resolve order-related issues. 


Inbox & Ticket Management


Manage inbound support tickets, emails, and live chat inquiries in a timely and professional manner. 
Prioritize customer issues based on business impact, urgency, and service level commitments. 
Maintain accurate ticket documentation and ensure customer inquiries are fully resolved. 
Meet established SLAs for first response time, resolution time, and ticket quality. 
Effectively manage multiple priorities while maintaining exceptional accuracy. 


Client Account Support


Support onboarding activities for new client accounts, including account setup and verification. 
Maintain accurate customer records, contact information, delivery details, and account preferences. 
Develop familiarity with assigned customer accounts and their operational requirements. 
Support day-to-day client communication and account management activities. 


Escalation Management


Gather complete order, shipment, and account information before escalating issues. 
Clearly document issues and provide detailed context for internal stakeholders. 
Follow up on escalated cases to ensure timely resolution and customer communication. 
Escalate operational risks and service failures appropriately. 


Customer Communication


Respond to inquiries related to orders, billing, deliveries, products, and account management. 
Deliver professional, empathetic, and solution-oriented written communication. 
Utilize approved SOPs, templates, macros, and knowledge base resources. 
Provide proactive communication during service disruptions, delays, and operational incidents. 


Process Improvement & Knowledge Management


Utilize internal documentation and knowledge base resources to resolve customer inquiries. 
Identify recurring customer issues, operational trends, and process gaps. 
Recommend improvements to processes and internal documentation. 
Participate in testing and refining new workflows, tools, and operational processes. 


Requirements:


3+ years of experience in Customer Support, Client Services, Customer Success, Account Support, or similar customer-facing roles. 
Experience managing high-volume support inboxes while maintaining exceptional attention to detail. 
Live chat support experience is required. 
Strong written and verbal English communication skills. 
Experience supporting business clients or complex customer accounts is preferred. 
Experience using HubSpot or similar CRM platforms is strongly preferred. 
Experience using Shopify or similar order management systems is preferred. 
Proficiency with Google Workspace, Slack, Microsoft Office, and business communication tools. 
Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy. 
Strong sense of ownership and accountability. 
Excellent organizational and time management skills. 
Ability to prioritize multiple requests in a fast-paced environment. 
Strong problem-solving and critical-thinking abilities. 
Comfortable working independently in a remote environment. 
Ability to build strong relationships with business customers. 
Proactive mindset with the ability to identify issues before they escalate. 


Nice to Have:


Experience supporting B2B, Enterprise, Hospitality, Healthcare, Education, or Institutional clients. 
Experience working in Food, Logistics, Fulfillment, Subscription, or E-commerce companies. 
Experience managing a portfolio of customer accounts. 
Experience partnering closely with Operations, Supply Chain, or Logistics teams. 
Familiarity with HubSpot, Shopify, Zendesk, Gorgias, or similar customer support platforms. 


About us:

The company provides ready-to-heat meal solutions for organizations across corporate offices, healthcare facilities, schools, and residential communities. By combining operational excellence with exceptional customer service, the team ensures every client receives reliable support and seamless service throughout their experience.

Customer Experience plays a critical role in the business, serving as the bridge between clients and internal operational teams to ensure issues are resolved efficiently while maintaining the highest service standards.

About the job

Responsibilities
At TikTok, our Global Business Solutions (GBS) team plays a key role in generating revenue by promoting our advertising solutions, onboarding new clients, driving ad campaigns, and more. As the TikTok community grows at an unprecedented speed around the world, our GBS team leads groundbreaking projects that are changing the landscape of the advertising industry in real time.

About the role:
The Account Manager (what we call Client Solutions Manager (CSM)) will work closely with the sales team to provide support and expertise in meeting client objectives and providing best in class customer service to both internal and external clients. The CSM will be responsible for negotiating and optimizing complex opportunities and use data and analytics to build a consultative solution for our clients. They will establish and strengthen key client relationships with a focus on driving revenue, advertiser education & advertiser satisfaction. Success in this position requires a great focus on client service, ability to thrive in an ever-changing work environment, and provide custom solutions for advertisers leveraging our unique suite of ad products.

Responsibilities:
- Manage and grow client relationships with advertisers. This includes conducting regular business reviews and taking a consultative, seller approach to increase client investments, in collaboration with the Sales Team.
- Build trusted relationships with clients through world class service, as well as identifying and proactively recommending custom solutions and new market opportunities.
- Maintain up-to-date knowledge of TikTok features and trends, and serve as an external product expert, educating clients on new and existing TikTok ad solutions and advising on best practices to drive optimal performance and groundbreaking campaigns.
- Work closely with Technical Account Managers to organize, monitor and analyze campaign performance.
- Make proactive and data-driven recommendations to optimize advertising offerings and strategies to achieve sales targets.
- Collaborate with a diverse group of internal cross-functional teams including Ad Ops, Product and R&D to ensure successful campaign executions and troubleshoot client issues.
- Collect marketplace feedback and insights to guide our advertising strategy and ad offerings
- Work to manage custom marketing programs/sponsorships with other departments internally and lead the custom content campaigns to ensure assets are distributed and delivered on time.

Qualifications
Minimum Qualification(s)
- 5+ years of experience in account management/client services roles in digital marketing
- Experience working directly with brand or performance advertisers, and executing custom branded campaigns.
- Strong verbal and written communication skills, with ability to effectively translate technical language to non-technical stakeholders
- Experience in campaign management and setup
- Ability to analyze data and identify insights to assess campaign performance and provide recommendations.
- Experience with ad verification partners (IAS, DV, MOAT)
- High attention to detail, strong organizational skills, self-starter attitude, fast learner with critical thinking.

Preferred Qualification(s)
- Biddable network experience
- Experience analyzing campaign delivery, diagnosing and solving technical problems with product and engineering teams is a plus
- Experience working in a fast-paced environment
- Experience working with global partners in different time zones & cultures

About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.

Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.
We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.

TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives.
